name: Sudo Find PrivEsc (Easy)
story: You are auditing a Linux server where the student account was given a small sudo exception for routine file searches. The rule was intended to be safe because it allows only one binary and does not require a password. Your job is to verify that assumption. Enumerate the sudo policy, identify the allowed command, then demonstrate how a legitimate administration tool can become a root shell when sudo permissions are too broad.
objectives: Enumerate sudo rights, abuse a misconfigured allowed binary, and read /root/flag.txt.

steps.title: Enumerate sudo rights
steps.description: List commands the student user can execute with sudo.
steps.question: Which command is allowed with NOPASSWD sudo access?

hints.text: Look for a single allowed binary path.

steps.title: Abuse the allowed binary
steps.description: Use the allowed binary to execute a privileged shell command.
steps.question: What user are you after successful privilege escalation?

hints.text: GTFOBins has documented patterns for find + sudo.
Use find -exec to spawn an interactive privileged shell.

steps.title: Capture the flag
steps.description: Read the protected root flag after escalation.
steps.question: What is the captured flag?

hints.text: If your shell is root, cat /root/flag.txt should work.
